page: Amazon Web Services p5e.48xlarge is a GPU-accelerated instance designed for intensive computational workloads. It is built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor and powered by an AMD x86_64 processor with 192 virtual CPUs and 96 physical cores. The dedicated CPU cores operate at a baseline frequency of 3.6 GHz, providing substantial host-level processing power to support complex orchestration and data preparation tasks.

The primary hardware feature of this instance is its integration of eight NVIDIA Hopper H200 GPUs, which provide a combined total of 1128 GB of high-bandwidth video memory. To prevent bottlenecks between host memory and accelerator memory, the system is equipped with 2048.0 GB of system RAM, yielding a ratio of approximately 10.67 GB of system memory per vCPU.

For local data persistence and high-speed caching, the instance includes 30400 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. This massive storage capacity is paired with a baseline network bandwidth of 100 Gbps, facilitating rapid data transfers across distributed clusters.

Given the high density of specialized accelerators, system memory, and local NVMe storage, this instance represents a high-tier hardware class with a corresponding resource density profile. It is engineered for workloads such as large language model training, complex deep learning inference, massive data science pipelines, and high-performance computing simulations that require parallel GPU processing and substantial local storage capacity.
description: Amazon Web Services p5e.48xlarge is a high-density, GPU-accelerated instance built on the Nitro hypervisor. The system features an AMD x86_64 processor with 192 dedicated vCPUs operating at 3.6 GHz, paired with 2048.0 GB of system memory. Acceleration is driven by eight NVIDIA Hopper H200 GPUs delivering 1128 GB of total VRAM. For high-speed local data access, the instance is equipped with 30400 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, supported by a baseline network bandwidth of 100 Gbps. This hardware profile is designed for parallelized computational tasks, including large-scale deep learning, artificial intelligence model training, and complex scientific simulations. The high density of GPU memory and local storage makes it suitable for data-intensive workloads that require minimal latency between storage and accelerators.
og_description: Amazon Web Services p5e.48xlarge is a GPU-accelerated instance featuring 192 vCPUs, 2048 GB of RAM, eight NVIDIA Hopper H200 GPUs with 1128 GB of VRAM, and 30400 GB of local NVMe SSD storage.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services p5e.48xlarge features 192 vCPUs, eight NVIDIA H200 GPUs, 2048 GB RAM, and 30400 GB NVMe SSD storage for intensive GPU workloads.
tagline: A high-density acceleration platform combining eight Hopper GPUs, massive system memory, and extensive local NVMe SSD storage for parallel computing.
bullet_points: Eight NVIDIA Hopper H200 GPUs with 1128 GB total VRAM
192 dedicated AMD vCPUs operating at 3.6 GHz
2048.0 GB of system memory for large-scale data handling
30400 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage
100 Gbps baseline network bandwidth for cluster connectivity
Designed for deep learning training, AI inference, and scientific simulations
